name: 5G Hierarchy of Threats
description: FiGHT is a knowledge base of adversary tactics and techniques targeting 5G network systems. Structured similarly to MITRE ATT&CK, FiGHT enumerates the techniques an adversary might use against the 5G core, RAN (radio access network), management plane, and 5G-specific protocols (e.g., HTTP/2-based service-based architecture, SUCI/SUPI handling, network slicing). FiGHT extends or specializes ATT&CK techniques where 5G-specific variations apply.
relationToCloudSecurity: 5G cores are increasingly cloud-native (containers, microservices, Kubernetes). FiGHT techniques against the 5G core often map to underlying cloud-platform attack surface — FiGHT is the most relevant framework for operators threat-modeling cloud-hosted 5G workloads.
relationToAISecurity: FiGHT does not specifically address AI-targeting techniques. AI components in 5G (e.g., AI-driven network optimization, traffic classification) would be threat-modeled with a combination of FiGHT (network plane) and ATLAS (AI/ML plane).

license.notes: FiGHT content is published by MITRE under terms similar to ATT&CK (free use with attribution). Verify specific license language on the upstream page before redistribution.

lifecycle.notes: FiGHT is a newer MITRE knowledge base; release cadence less frequent than ATT&CK.

current_extraction.notes: Local fight.json/yaml are direct downloads of MITRE-published exports. No transformation applied.

scope.covers: 5G network adversary techniques (core, RAN, management plane, 5G-specific protocols)

notes: Niche but valuable for 5G operators. Often consumed alongside 3GPP security specifications.
